title: Health, safety and wellbeing - Ministry of Education
content:
## Health, safety and wellbeing
Find out how to protect children's health, safety and wellbeing in early learning services.
- Emergency preparedness 3
- Food and nutrition 1
- Ngā kaupapahere tiaki tamariki mā ngā ratonga kōhungahunga Child protection policies for early learning services All early learning services, and any person or organisation we contract to provide children’s services, must have a child protection policy.
All early learning services, and any person or organisation we contract to provide children’s services, must have a child protection policy.
- Ngā ohotata me ngā takunetanga tairaru ki ngā ratonga kōhungahunga Emergencies and serious incidents at an early learning service How to respond to and care for children, staff and your community if a child is seriously injured or ill, or there is another emergency.
How to respond to and care for children, staff and your community if a child is seriously injured or ill, or there is another emergency.
- Ngā tohu whakaora whawhati tata mā ngā ratonga kōhungahunga First aid qualifications for early learning All early learning services and kōhanga reo must have 1 first aid qualified person per 25 children.
All early learning services and kōhanga reo must have 1 first aid qualified person per 25 children.
- Te haumaru ā-kai ki rō ratonga kōhungahunga Food safety at early learning services Early learning services that cook meals or prepare food for children need to meet all food safety and nutrition requirements.
Early learning services that cook meals or prepare food for children need to meet all food safety and nutrition requirements.
- Te tuku rongoā ki ngā tamariki ki ngā ratonga ako kōhungahunga Giving medicine to children at early learning services Early learning services must be able to give medicine to children who need it because they are sick or have a disability. This is part of your responsibility to reasonably accommodate all children.
Early learning services must be able to give medicine to children who need it because they are sick or have a disability. This is part of your responsibility to reasonably accommodate all children.
- Te tiaki i tētahi pēpi e tangi ana ki te ako kōhungahunga Looking after a crying baby in early learning If a baby is crying continually in your early learning service, you should stay calm and ask for support.
If a baby is crying continually in your early learning service, you should stay calm and ask for support.
- Ngā tātarihanga Pirihimana mā ngā ratonga kōhungahunga Police vetting for early learning services Police vets for children's workers are part of the safety checking process all early learning services must complete.
Police vets for children's workers are part of the safety checking process all early learning services must complete.
- Te whakarite mō te tūpono ohotata, mō te tūpono pāmamaetanga rānei ki rō whare kōhungahunga Prepare for an emergency or traumatic incident in early learning All early learning services must have an emergency management plan in place.
All early learning services must have an emergency management plan in place.
- Te tiaki i ngā tamariki ki ngā ratonga kōhungahunga Protecting children at early learning services Regulations and licensing criteria set out what you must do to protect the safety and wellbeing of children at your early learning service.
Regulations and licensing criteria set out what you must do to protect the safety and wellbeing of children at your early learning service.
- Ngā aromātai mōrearea i muri o te tātarihanga Pirihimana ki rō whare kōhungahunga Risk assessments after a Police vet for early learning services Early learning service providers must do a risk assessment after a Police vet for non-children’s workers who will have unsupervised access to children.
Early learning service providers must do a risk assessment after a Police vet for non-children’s workers who will have unsupervised access to children.
- Ngā hihiratanga hei tiaki tamariki ki rō whare kōhungahunga Safety checking for child protection in early learning All children's workers employed or engaged by a service must be safety checked.
All children's workers employed or engaged by a service must be safety checked.
- Te whakapuaki i ngā pārongo whaiaro hei tiaki tamariki ki rō whare kōhungahunga Sharing personal information to protect children in early learning Your early learning service may need to share personal information with other agencies, such as the Police, to protect children.
Your early learning service may need to share personal information with other agencies, such as the Police, to protect children.
